gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/217)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/217)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ يَتِيهُ إِلَّا مِنْ ذِلَّةٍ يَجِدُهَا فِي نَفْسِهِ
وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ مَا مِنْ رَجُلٍ تَكَبَّرَ أَوْ تَجَبَّرَ إِلَّا لِذِلَّةٍ وَجَدَهَا فِي نَفْسِهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from one of his companions, from al Nahdy, from Yazeed Bin Is’haq Sha’ar, from Abdullah Bin Al Munzir, from Abdullah Bin Bukeyr who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no one who comes to it (arrogance) except from a disgrace which he finds within himself’.

And in another Hadeeth, from Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> having said: ‘There is none from a man who is arrogant or tyrannous except due to the disgrace which he finds within himself’.
en.sarwar: 17. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from certain individuals of his people from al-Nahdi from Yazid ibn Ishaq Sha’ar from ‘Abd Allah ibn al-Mudar from ‘Abd Allah ibn Bukayr who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘No one expresses arrogance but because of lowliness that he feels in his soul.’ In another Hadith abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘There is no man who expresses arrogance or tyranny but because of lowliness that he feels in his soul.”
